channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - insider-gaming.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for insider-gaming.com

item.title: Today’s Contexto #1270: Hints & Answer For March 11, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Contexto is a word association game that gives you a new word to guess every single day. With every attempt, the AI algorithm determines how close your guess is to the daily answer based on thematic and contextual similarity.

Sometimes, though, no matter how experienced you are, the word you’re looking for can be incredibly vague and hard to deduce. Luckily for you, this guide is full of hints to give you a helping hand, but make sure you give today’s Contexto a go if you haven’t already!

How To Play Contexto

Contexto is incredibly simple to play, but difficult to master. All you have to do is type words into the search bar, and the AI algorithm will tell you how close your word is to the answer.

You’ll know how close your guess is based on the colour of the bar and the number next to it.

  • Red includes guesses with the number 1,501 and above, meaning you’re way off from the secret word.
  • Orange includes guesses between 301 and 1,500, meaning you’re getting closer, but still some ways off.
  • Green includes guesses between 1 and 300, meaning you’re on the right track.

Best Contexto Tips & Tricks

  • Start off using broad terms, then narrow down once you’ve got under the 1,000 mark.
  • Try using Nouns first, and dip into verbs or adjectives once you’ve discovered the context.
  • Experiment with random words at times, especially as different forms of a root word can rank differently, such as work and worker.
  • Guess away, as unlike other word games like Wordle, you aren’t limited to a strict number of attempts.

Best Starting Words For Contexto

Here are some great starting words to get you going with Contexto:

  • Thing
  • Person
  • Place
  • Animal
  • Work
  • Nature
  • Game
  • Concept
  • Idea
  • Food

Contexto Hints For Today, March 11, 2026

Without giving away today’s Contexto answer, here are a few helpful hints to get you thinking in the right direction:

  • Hint 1: A form of study
  • Hint 2: Concerns life and living organisms
  • Hint 3: One of the three main fields of science

If they don’t help, then here are the nine closest words, going from tenth to second:

  • Immunology
  • Neuroscience
  • Genetic
  • Chemistry
  • Microbiology
  • Science
  • Ecology
  • Physiology
  • Biochemistry

Today’s Contexto Answer For Game #1270 On March 11, 2026

If you’ve tried everything and still can’t figure out today’s Contexto answer, then you can find it down below:

Answer: Biology

Biology is the scientific study of life and living organisms.

When Is The Next Contexto Game After #1270?

Contexto is updated daily at 12 AM or Midnight in your respective time zone, meaning the next game will start on March 12.

The Contexto schedule is very consistent, with a new game releasing at the same time every day.

How did you find today’s Contexto? Share your thoughts in the official Insider Gaming Discord Server.

MOBILE APP

item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: LEGO Leak Claims PlayStation 1 Console Set Coming This Year
item.content:encoded:
It has been claimed by a prominent LEGO leaker that a PlayStation console set, including ‘at least’ a controller and console, is incoming and will land this December. The set will be based on a classic PlayStation console and not the smaller variant that was released later.

Until now, LEGO fans have relied on self-made replications to explore their collaborative love of both PlayStation and LEGO. It’s worth stressing that, while the leaker is reliable, everything below should be taken with a pinch of salt.

LEGO PlayStation Console Coming Soon

On Instagram, user lego_minecraft_goat posted an image of ‘LEGO Codename P or Play’, revealing that it’s a replication of a classic PlayStation console, released in the mid-1990s.

The information given included the typical numerical code (72306) and information about the set size. It was claimed the LEGO PlayStation will feature 1911 pieces, and that when it drops in December, it’ll come with a $159.99 price tag.

(Special thanks to brickleaks_ for the spot)

If you run a cursory search for ‘LEGO PlayStation’, you’ll see a fan-made submission on the LEGO IDEAS platform. It was raised in June last year, and it shows someone’s 885-piece build that features a classic PlayStation console, two controllers (with wiring), and two memory cards.

At the time of writing, 5,338 people had supported the idea, but given the timeline, it’s likely LEGO was cooking up the real PlayStation set before this was proposed.

If it does exist, the PlayStation LEGO set will follow nicely in the footsteps of the Game Boy set, released late last year and priced at around $59.99.

Would you invest $159.99 in a LEGO PlayStation set? Let us know what you’re thinking on the Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more Insider Gaming coverage, check out the news that ARC Raiders is refunding lost loadouts

Written by

Senior Editor and Esports Lead

Grant's adoration for gaming started on the Amstrad CPC in 1996. Now, he's a multi-platform expert gamer with a deep love for the industry, a passion for esports, and more…

More from Grant Taylor-Hill

MOBILE APP

item.title: Cloud9 New York Signs Kenny After a Month in the Cold
item.content:encoded:
Kenneth ‘Kenny’ Williams has found a spot on Cloud9 New York after more than a month outside of a roster spot in the Call of Duty League. The two-time (almost three-time) world champion was dropped by LA Thieves in February after a weak start to the 2026 season, a move that came after a rough stint following his 2024 CDL Champs win with OpTic Texas.

Between lifting the COD Champs trophy and signing with Cloud9 New York, Kenny crashed in the KD stakes, had a hugely unsuccessful period with Minnesota ROKKR, and had just a few weeks in the League with LAT before being cast out.

Kenny Has a New Home

In a tongue-in-cheek post on social media, Cloud9 New York revealed the signing of Kenny. It was in response to a divisive, controversial page known as ‘CDL Highlight’, making a rough comment about Kenny back on March 4th:

I’ll permanently delete my Twitter account if you end up back on a CDL team.

You will never be back in the league you bum

The C9NY social team wrote:

Our end of the deal is done

It’s a tricky place to be, though. Cloud9 New York is currently flat bottom in the CDL standings for the 2026 season. The team, which looked as though it would be roster-less for a while, has managed to win just two out of 15 matches this year. The map win rate is a paltry 32.7%

At the other end of the table, for comparison, you have OpTic Texas, Kenny’s former organization. That squad is sitting on a win record of 15 – 4 and a map win score of 70%. So, Kenny is back in the League, sure, but it’s not in the most advantageous position possible.

Cinderella story coming up at Major II in the United Kingdom? Maybe.

Are you surprised that C9NY has signed Kenny? Let us know what you’re thinking on the Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more Insider Gaming coverage, check out the news about the recent League of Legends rework

Written by

Senior Editor and Esports Lead

Grant's adoration for gaming started on the Amstrad CPC in 1996. Now, he's a multi-platform expert gamer with a deep love for the industry, a passion for esports, and more…

More from Grant Taylor-Hill

item.title: League of Legends Shyvanna Rework Fixes Her Most Frustrating Feature
item.content:encoded:
The Shyvanna rework comes to League of Legends next week, and the visual game update (VGU) should make the Demacian champion feel much better to play when it releases on LoL Patch 26.6.

Riot Games is finally releasing the highly anticipated update to the top laner/jungler, which was originally released back in 2011. The VGU will give Shyvana brand new abilities and upgrade her character model and skins.

The rework has been playable on the LoL Public Beta Environment (PBE) for a little while now, and players have discovered a small, unspoken quality-of-life update to Shyvana’s kit, which will make her feel significantly better to play.

LoL Shyvana VGU Quality of Life Upgrade

The newly reworked Shyvana will still feel very familiar to old mains of the half-dragon, especially as she is a manaless champion who instead generates Fury stacks. At maximum Fury, Shyvana will still turn into a dragon and gain increased health and enhancements to her abilities.

Previously, unlike some other champions with non-mana resources, this Fury resource was only generated while Shyvana was alive.

This meant that if a Shyvana player died during an important fight and then needed to defend their base, they would respawn with zero Fury and wouldn’t have long enough to stack it for their crucial ultimate.

Riot Games has fixed this with the new update, allowing the champion to passively generate Fury at all times, even while dead. Shyvana is a champion who relies heavily on her ultimate, so being able to use it sooner after respawn will mean she won’t be automatically useless when defending her team’s Nexus.

To compensate for this, the Fury regeneration will be somewhat slower for the reworked version.

Shyvana’s Reworked LoL Abilities

Passive – Scalemail

Shyvana generates stacks of Scalemail by scoring takedowns (kills and assists). Large monsters and large minions grant one stack, while enemy champions and epic monsters grant three stacks.

Shyvana gains 0.4 bonus armor and 0.4 magic resist for each stack.

Q – Emberstrike

Passively, Shyvana’s autoattacks deal bonus magic damage. When activated, Emberstrike empowers her next autoattack to have increased range and deal damage in a cone. After activating the first time, Emberstrike can quickly be recast a second time soon after.

Shyvana’s basic attacks reduce the remaining cooldown of this ability.

When in her Dragon Form, Emberstrike gets an additional recast, which also deals true damage.

W – Inferno Aegis

Shyvana gains a shield for 2.5 seconds, which gets 30% stronger for every nearby enemy champion. She also gains bonus movement speed while facing visible opponents.

The shield detonates once it expires or the ability is recast to deal AoE damage.

In Dragon Form, Shyvana also gets a substantial heal if the detonation hits an enemy champion.

E – Molten Burst

Shyvana launches a fireball that deals magic damage and explodes at maximum range or when it hits an enemy champion.

In Dragon Form, this projectile is faster, wider, penetrates enemy champions to hit multiple targets, deals 25% increased damage, and leaves a damaging effect on the ground for two seconds.

R – Dragon’s Descent

Passively, Shyvana generates Dragon Fury per second while in her Human Form. Her basic attacks and abilities generate another 1.25 Fury for every enemy champion hit per cast — this is increased by 200% in her Dragon Form.

Once Shyvana’s Fury bar is full at 100 Dragon Fury, she can turn into her Dragon Form, flying forward with displacement immunity and dealing damage in a wave that also fears enemies.

In this form, Shyvana gains bonus health, attack range, and size as well as enhancements to all her active abilities. The Dragon Form costs Fury, and once her Fury bar depletes, she returns to her human form.

Are you excited for the Shyvana VGU? Let us know on the Insider Gaming Discord

And check out all the details about the LEC Spring Split, where the new version of Shyvana will make its esports debut.

MOBILE APP
